note_data.title: Reversible reactions and equilibrium
keyPoints: A **reversible reaction** is one that can proceed in both the forward and backward directions simultaneously
At **dynamic equilibrium**, the rate of forward reaction equals the rate of backward reaction
The **Haber process** produces ammonia: N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃ (exothermic reaction)
Haber process conditions: **450°C temperature, 200 atmospheres pressure, iron catalyst**
The **Contact process** produces sulfur trioxide: 2SO₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2SO₃ (exothermic reaction)
Contact process conditions: **450°C temperature, 1-2 atmospheres pressure, vanadium(V) oxide catalyst**
**Le Chatelier's principle**: if conditions change, equilibrium shifts to oppose the change
Increasing pressure favours the side with **fewer gaseous molecules**
**Catalysts** speed up both forward and backward reactions equally, so equilibrium position is unchanged

definitions.definition: A chemical reaction that can proceed in both forward and backward directions
The state where the rate of forward reaction equals the rate of backward reaction, and concentrations remain constant
If a system at equilibrium is subjected to a change, the equilibrium will shift to oppose that change
A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being permanently changed

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State what happens + apply Le Chatelier's principle to justify

modelAnswer.question: The Haber process is used to manufacture ammonia. State the balanced equation and explain why high pressure is used in this process.
modelAnswer.answer: N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃. High pressure is used because there are 4 molecules of reactants but only 2 molecules of product. According to Le Chatelier's principle, high pressure favours the side with fewer molecules, so equilibrium shifts right to produce more ammonia.
markBreakdown: Mark 4: Le Chatelier's principle correctly applied

commonMistakes: Never use → for reversible reactions — always use ⇌ symbol
Students often say 'equilibrium stops' — it's dynamic so reactions continue at equal rates
Don't say catalysts 'shift equilibrium' — they only affect the rate of reaching equilibrium
Never confuse Haber (ammonia) with Contact (sulfur trioxide) process conditions
Students forget that fertiliser production requires ammonia to be converted to ammonium compounds
examinerTips: Reversible reactions appear in every Paper 2 — know both industrial processes thoroughly
Always include the ⇌ symbol when writing reversible reaction equations
Link industrial processes to Pakistan's agricultural needs for fertilisers
Remember that compromise conditions are used in industry to balance yield and rate
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Energy level diagram showing forward and backward reaction pathways with activation energies, illustrating how catalysts lower activation energy for both directions equally
